content.content: Launching skill: gsd:execute-phase

content.text:
<objective>
Execute all plans in a phase using wave-based parallel execution.

Orchestrator stays lean: discover plans, analyze dependencies, group into waves, spawn subagents, collect results. Each subagent loads the full execute-plan context and handles its own plan.

Context budget: ~15% orchestrator, 100% fresh per subagent.
</objective>

    64→Recent decisions affecting v3.0:
    65→- **Aspire + K8s parallel paths**: Aspire stays as local inner-loop dev tool; Kustomize + ArgoCD define cluster state. No conflict.
    66→- **RabbitMQ as K8s transport**: Azure SB emulator cannot run in K8s; `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT` env var switches transport. Same consumer/saga/outbox config.
    67→- **Kustomize over Helm**: Simpler for single-project; no templating language; built into kubectl.

    69→- **Sealed Secrets from day one**: No plaintext credentials in Git ever. Key backup in cluster bootstrap script.
    70→- **NodePort for kind access**: Ingress NGINX EOL March 2026; NodePort is simplest for local kind cluster.
    71→- **No MinIO**: Azurite not suitable for K8s; use placeholder images in K8s deployment.
    72→- [Phase 23]: Next.js Dockerfile uses AUTH_SECRET=placeholder-for-docker-build to prevent next-auth v5 build failures; real secret injected at K8s runtime

    74→- [Phase 23]: Separate container-images.yml workflow from release.yml -- GITHUB_TOKEN for ghcr.io auth, .NET multi-arch via per-arch publish + imagetools merge
    75→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: PGDATA subdirectory avoids initdb directory-not-empty error from kind local-path-provisioner
    76→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: Offset ports (3xxxx) for kind cluster avoid conflicts with Aspire local dev services
    77→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: Separate headless + NodePort services for PostgreSQL -- headless for StatefulSet DNS, NodePort for kind port mapping chain
    78→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Keycloak startup probe on management port 9000 with 15s initial delay and 60s probing window for slow first-boot realm import
    79→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Realm JSON copied from AppHost/Realms to K8s manifests for self-contained deployment; configMapGenerator for hash-suffixed ConfigMap
    80→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Separate NodePort services for kind external access; KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME/PASSWORD (not deprecated KEYCLOAK_ADMIN)
    81→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: SealedSecrets v0.27.3 pinned for reproducible controller install; sealed-secret.yaml generated dynamically by bootstrap script
    82→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: seal_secret helper function centralizes kubeseal invocation; dev defaults postgres/guest/admin for local-only kind cluster
    83→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: Bootstrap script idempotent (skips existing cluster); Keycloak 180s pod wait timeout for realm import
    84→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-04]: ROADMAP criterion #5 corrected from ApiService to Keycloak startup probe; ApiService probe deferred to Phase 25 criterion #6
    85→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT env var controls transport: RabbitMQ for K8s, AzureServiceBus (default) for Aspire
    86→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: NoOp DLQ service returns empty results in RabbitMQ mode; health endpoints unconditional for K8s probes
    87→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: CORS origins configurable via Cors:Origins config section with localhost fallback
    88→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: Env var substitution pattern: define secretKeyRef env vars BEFORE connection strings so K8s $(VAR) substitution works

1→# Phase 25.1: Update UI based on pencil design with shadcn - Context
     2→

     6→<domain>
     7→## Phase Boundary
     8→
     9→Refresh all storefront and admin UI screens to match the split per-page pencil design files using existing shadcn/ui primitives and Tailwind CSS. This is a visual update of existing screens — no new backend features, API endpoints, or routes are added. The `design/*.pen` files are the source of truth for layout, colors, and component styling.
    10→
    11→</domain>

    13→<decisions>
    14→## Implementation Decisions
    15→
    16→### Design file strategy
    17→- Source of truth: per-page `.pen` files in `design/` directory (11 files total)
    18→- `design/design-system.pen` for shared component specs (26 reusable components) and color variables
    19→- Each screen references its own `.pen` file (e.g., `design/storefront-homepage.pen` for homepage)
    20→- Old monolithic `pencil-new.pen` is deleted — split files replace it entirely
    21→- Design fidelity: match layout structure, color palette, component style from pencil. Use shadcn defaults for spacing/radius where close enough. Don't chase pixel-perfect
    22→
    23→### Component strategy
    24→- Use shadcn/ui for standard components (Button, Badge, Input, Table, Avatar, Tabs, etc.) — theme them to match design
    25→- Create custom components only for things shadcn doesn't cover: CategoryPill, StarRating, QuantityStepper, StepIndicator, ProductCard
    26→- Custom feature components in `src/components/storefront/`, reusable primitives in `src/components/ui/`
    27→- Rewrite existing storefront components to match design — keep same file names and prop interfaces where possible, JSX/styling gets fresh start
    28→- Install additional shadcn components as needed per screen (e.g., tabs, pagination, breadcrumb). Don't pre-install everything
    29→
    30→### Font & typography
    31→- Primary font: DM Sans via next/font/google (matches design-system.pen exactly)
    32→- Fallback: Inter, then system sans-serif
    33→- Load weights 400 (normal), 500 (medium), 600 (semibold) only — matches design usage, keeps bundle small
    34→- Single font stack for entire app — no separate display font for headings
    35→- Replace current Geist Sans references with DM Sans
    36→
    37→### Color theme migration
    38→- Convert design hex colors to oklch format in globals.css — maintains Tailwind v4 + shadcn best practice
    39→- Primary blue: #2563EB (oklch equivalent), foreground: #1A1A1A, border: #E5E7EB, etc.
    40→- Extend CSS variables with semantic status colors: --success, --success-bg, --success-foreground (and warning/error/info equivalents)
    41→- Add --star (#FBBF24 amber) as a theme variable for star ratings
    42→- Admin sidebar: override --sidebar-bg (#111827), --sidebar-foreground (#F9FAFB), --sidebar-accent (#1F2937) via CSS custom properties
    43→- No dark mode — light mode only. Design only shows light mode
    44→- Remove .dark section from globals.css (or keep inert)
    45→
    46→### Screen priority & scope
    47→- All 10 screens + theme/globals setup = 11 implementation units
    48→- One plan per screen for clear progress tracking
    49→- Implementation order (customer journey):
    50→  1. Theme/globals (CSS variables, font, shared component theming)
    51→  2. Homepage (`design/storefront-homepage.pen`)
    52→  3. Product Catalog (`design/storefront-catalog.pen`)
    53→  4. Product Detail (`design/storefront-product-detail.pen`)
    54→  5. Shopping Cart (`design/storefront-cart.pen`)
    55→  6. Checkout (`design/storefront-checkout.pen`)
    56→  7. Order Confirmation (`design/storefront-order-confirmation.pen`)
    57→  8. Order History (`design/storefront-order-history.pen`)
    58→  9. User Profile (`design/storefront-profile.pen`)
    59→  10. Auth Login (`design/auth-login.pen`)
    60→  11. Admin Dashboard (`design/admin-dashboard.pen`)
    61→
    62→### Responsive approach
    63→- Desktop-first: build 1440px layout from pencil, add sensible breakpoints
    64→- Stack grids to single column, collapse sidebar to sheet/drawer, adjust padding
    65→- Admin sidebar: shared layout component in admin `layout.tsx` as persistent navigation shell
    66→
    67→### Claude's Discretion
    68→- Exact responsive breakpoint values and mobile layout adjustments
    69→- Loading/skeleton states per screen
    70→- Error state handling
    71→- Animation/transitions between states
    72→- Exact shadcn component selection per screen
    73→- Which components are "reusable primitives" (ui/) vs "feature components" (storefront/)
    74→- Per-component decision on refactor vs rewrite based on complexity
    75→- Exact oklch conversion values for each hex color
    76→
    77→</decisions>

The work is purely presentational -- no new backend features, API endpoints, or routes are added. Existing React component file names and prop interfaces are preserved where possible; only the JSX/styling gets rewritten. 59→ 60→The current codebase uses Next.js 16.0.3, React 19.2, Tailwind CSS v4.1.17, and shadcn/ui (new-york style, v3.8.5 CLI). There are 18 shadcn/ui components already installed, 23 storefront components, and a well-structured app router layout. The main changes are: (1) swap the color system from default shadcn grayscale to the design's blue-primary palette in oklch, (2) replace Geist Sans with DM Sans font, (3) add custom semantic status variables, (4) add missing shadcn components (tabs, pagination, breadcrumb, checkbox, slider, sidebar), and (5) rewrite each screen's JSX to match the design layout. 61→ 62→**Primary recommendation:** Implement as 11 sequential plans (theme first, then each screen in customer-journey order). Each plan reads its `.pen` design file, installs any needed shadcn components, and rewrites the screen's components to match the design. The theme/globals plan is the foundation -- all subsequent screens depend on it. 63→ 64→## Standard Stack 65→ 66→### Core (Already Installed) 67→| Library | Version | Purpose | Why Standard | Architecture Patterns 112→ 113→### Recommended Project Structure (No Changes to Existing Structure) 114→``` 115→src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/ 116→├── app/ 117→│ ├── globals.css # Theme variables (REWRITE) 118→│ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout: DM Sans font (EDIT) 119→│ ├── (storefront)/ 120→│ │ ├── layout.tsx # Header + Footer shell (EDIT) 121→│ │ ├── page.tsx # Homepage (EDIT) 122→│ │ ├── products/[id]/page.tsx # Product detail (EDIT) 123→│ │ ├── cart/page.tsx # Cart (EDIT) 124→│ │ ├── checkout/page.tsx # Checkout (EDIT) 125→│ │ ├── order-confirmation/ # Order confirmation (EDIT) 126→│ (ADD new ones) 132→│ ├── storefront/ # Feature components (REWRITE) 133→│ ├── reviews/ # Review components (RESTYLE) 134→│ ├── account/ # Account components (RESTYLE) 135→│ ├── admin/ # Admin components (RESTYLE) 136→│ └── wishlist/ # Wishlist components (RESTYLE) 137→├── hooks/ # Data fetching hooks (NO CHANGES) 138→└── lib/ # API client, utils (NO CHANGES) 139→``` 140→ 141→### Pattern 1: CSS Variable Theme Swap 142→**What:** Replace all `:root` CSS custom properties in globals.css with design-system.pen color values converted to oklch. Add new semantic variables (--success, --warning, --error, --info, --star) to the @theme inline block. 143→**When to use:** Plan 1 (Theme/Globals) -- foundation for all other screens. 144→**Example:** 145→```css 146→/* Source: design-system.pen variables + shadcn theming docs */ 147→@import "tailwindcss"; 148→@import "tw-animate-css"; 149→ 150→@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *)); 151→ 152→@theme inline { 153→ --color-background: var(--background); 154→ --color-foreground: var(--foreground); 155→ --font-sans: var(--font-dm-sans); 156→ /* ... existing shadcn mappings ... */ 157→ /* NEW: semantic status colors */ 158→ --color-success: var(--success); 159→ --color-success-bg: var(--success-bg); 160→ --color-success-foreground: var(--success-foreground); 161→ --color-warning: var(--warning); 162→ --color-warning-bg: var(--warning-bg); 163→ --color-warning-foreground: var(--warning-foreground); 164→ --color-error: var(--error); 165→ --color-error-bg: var(--error-bg); 166→ --color-error-foreground: var(--error-foreground); 167→ --color-info: var(--info); 168→ --color-info-bg: var(--info-bg); 169→ --color-info-foreground: var(--info-foreground); 170→ --color-star: var(--star); 171→} 172→ 173→:root { 174→ --radius: 0.625rem; 175→ --background: oklch(1 0 0); /* #FFFFFF */ 176→ --foreground: oklch(0.156 0 0); /* #1A1A1A */ 177→ --primary: oklch(0.546 0.245 262.881); /* #2563EB */ 178→ --primary-foreground: oklch(1 0 0); /* #FFFFFF */ 179→ --border: oklch(0.928 0.006 264.531); /* #E5E7EB */ 180→ /* ... full palette ... */ 181→ --star: oklch(0.828 0.189 84.429); /* #FBBF24 */ 182→ --success: oklch(0.723 0.219 149.579); /* #22C55E */ 183→ --success-bg: oklch(0.982 0.018 155.826); /* #F0FDF4 */ 184→ --success-foreground: oklch(0.393 0.095 152.535); /* #166534 */ 185→ /* ... etc. ... */ 186→} 187→``` 188→ 189→### Pattern 2: Font Swap via next/font/google 190→**What:** Replace Geist Sans with DM Sans using next/font/google in root layout.tsx 191→**When to use:** Plan 1 (Theme/Globals) 192→**Example:** 193→```tsx 194→// Source: Next.js v16.0.3 next/font/google docs (Context7) 195→import { DM_Sans } from "next/font/google"; 196→ 197→const dmSans = DM_Sans({ 198→ subsets: ["latin"], 199→ weight: ["400", "500", "600"], 200→ variable: "--font-dm-sans", 201→ display: "swap", 202→}); 203→ 204→export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { 205→ return ( 206→ 207→ {children} 208→ 209→ ); 210→} 211→``` 212→Then in globals.css `@theme inline`: `--font-sans: var(--font-dm-sans);` 213→ 214→### Pattern 3: Screen Component Rewrite 215→**What:** For each screen, read the .pen file, map design elements to shadcn/React components, rewrite the JSX while preserving the existing prop interface and data hooks. 216→**When to use:** Plans 2-11 (each screen) 217→**Key principle:** Keep the same file name and exported component name. Keep the same hooks and data-fetching logic. Only change the return JSX and Tailwind classes. 218→ 219→### Pattern 4: Admin Sidebar Layout 220→**What:** Replace the current top-nav admin layout with a dark sidebar layout using shadcn's Sidebar component. The sidebar uses custom CSS variables for the dark navy theme. 221→**When to use:** Plan 11 (Admin Dashboard) 222→**Example:** 223→```tsx 224→// In admin/layout.tsx or a wrapper component 225→import { SidebarProvider, Sidebar, SidebarContent, SidebarGroup, SidebarMenuItem } from "@/components/ui/sidebar"; 226→ 227→// Sidebar-specific overrides via CSS custom properties on wrapper 228→
229→ 230→ ... 231→
{children}
232→ 233→
234→``` 235→ 236→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid 237→- **Don't change hook logic during visual refactor:** Hooks (useCart, useCheckout, useOrders, etc.) remain untouched. Only JSX and className change. 238→- **Don't hardcode colors as hex in JSX:** Always use CSS variables via Tailwind utilities (`bg-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`). The design's hex values go into globals.css only. 239→- **Don't pre-install all shadcn components:** Install only what the current plan's screen needs. Avoids unused code and makes it clear which screen introduced which component. 240→- **Don't break existing route structure:** No file renames, no new routes, no moving pages. This is visual-only. 241→- **Don't import from design/ at runtime:** .pen files are design-time reference only, never imported in application code. 242→ 243→## Don't Hand-Roll 244→ 245→| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why | 246→|---------|-------------|-------------|-----| 247→| Tab panels | Custom tab state management | `shadcn tabs` (@radix-ui/react-tabs) | Keyboard nav, ARIA roles, focus management | 248→| Pagination | Custom page number logic | `shadcn pagination` | Proper prev/next/ellipsis, a11y | 249→| Breadcrumbs | Custom trail rendering | `shadcn breadcrumb` | Semantic nav, separator, ARIA | 250→| Checkbox groups | Raw `` | `shadcn checkbox` (@radix-ui/react-checkbox) | Indeterminate state, ARIA, consistent styling | 251→| Range slider | Custom drag handler | `shadcn slider` (@radix-ui/react-slider) | Touch/pointer events, keyboard, ARIA | 252→| Sidebar navigation | Custom CSS sidebar | `shadcn sidebar` | Responsive collapse, keyboard, sheet on mobile | 253→| oklch color conversion | Manual math | oklch.com or CSS Color Level 4 tools | Human error in manual conversion; tooling is fast and accurate | 254→| Font loading | `` tags in `` | next/font/google DM_Sans | Auto-optimization, no CLS, self-hosting, CSS variable output | 255→ 256→**Key insight:** shadcn provides accessible, keyboard-navigable, ARIA-compliant primitives. Hand-rolling these for a visual refresh is wasted effort and introduces accessibility regressions. 257→ 258→## Common Pitfalls 259→ 260→### Pitfall 1: Primary Color Becomes Blue But shadcn Defaults Assume Grayscale 261→**What goes wrong:** shadcn's default `--primary` is a near-black grayscale. Switching to blue (#2563EB) means hover states like `hover:bg-primary/90` produce a slightly transparent blue instead of a darker shade. Text contrast ratios may shift. 262→**Why it happens:** The oklch color space handles lightness differently than hex. 90% opacity of a saturated blue on white background looks different than 90% of near-black. 263→**How to avoid:** After setting --primary to blue oklch, verify all Button variants visually. If hover states look washed out, define an explicit `--primary-hover` or adjust the opacity value in button.tsx cva variants. 264→**Warning signs:** Buttons that look "faded" on hover rather than "pressed/darkened." 265→ 266→### Pitfall 2: Font Weight Mismatch Between Design and Code 267→**What goes wrong:** DM Sans loaded with only weights 400/500/600, but design uses `fontWeight: "bold"` (700) somewhere, causing the browser to synthesize bold from 600 -- looks slightly different. 268→**Why it happens:** Design files may use "bold" generically. DM Sans 600 (semibold) is the heaviest loaded weight. 269→**How to avoid:** Audit the .pen files for any fontWeight > 600 before implementation. If weight 700 appears, add it to the next/font/google config. From the design-system.pen data reviewed, all weights are 400/500/600 -- safe. 270→**Warning signs:** Text appearing slightly thicker or thinner than the design mockup. 271→ 272→### Pitfall 3: @theme inline Variables Not Picking Up New CSS Custom Properties 273→**What goes wrong:** Adding `--success` to `:root` but forgetting to add `--color-success: var(--success)` to the `@theme inline` block means `bg-success` Tailwind class won't work. 274→**Why it happens:** Tailwind v4 requires both the CSS custom property definition AND the @theme inline mapping for the utility to be generated. 275→**How to avoid:** For every new CSS variable added to `:root`, add the corresponding `--color-{name}: var(--{name})` to `@theme inline`. Verify with `bg-success` in a test element. 276→**Warning signs:** Tailwind classes like `bg-success` producing no styles (silently ignored). 277→ 278→### Pitfall 4: Admin Sidebar CSS Variable Scope Bleeding 279→**What goes wrong:** Overriding `--sidebar` variables globally affects the storefront sidebar/sheet components too. 280→**Why it happens:** If sidebar variables are set at `:root` level instead of scoped to the admin layout. 281→**How to avoid:** Scope admin sidebar overrides to the admin layout wrapper element using inline style or a CSS class selector like `.admin-layout { --sidebar: oklch(...); }`. 282→**Warning signs:** Storefront mobile drawer/sheet using dark navy colors instead of light theme. 283→ 284→### Pitfall 5: E2E Tests Breaking Due to Selector Changes 285→**What goes wrong:** Playwright E2E tests use selectors like `a[href*="/products/"]`, `getByRole('heading')`, and `getByPlaceholder(/search/i)`. If component structure changes, selectors break. 286→**Why it happens:** Visual rewrites can change DOM nesting, element types, or remove/rename elements. 287→**How to avoid:** Keep semantic HTML elements (headings, links, buttons, inputs). Add `data-testid` attributes if needed. After each screen rewrite, run relevant E2E tests if the Aspire stack is available. 288→**Warning signs:** E2E locators failing to find elements after component rewrite. 289→ 290→### Pitfall 6: Geist Font CSS Variable Still Referenced 291→**What goes wrong:** `--font-geist-sans` and `--font-geist-mono` still referenced in globals.css `@theme inline` after font swap, causing fallback to system fonts unexpectedly. 292→**Why it happens:** Incomplete cleanup of the old font configuration. 293→**How to avoid:** Search for all references to `geist` in the codebase and replace. Key files: `globals.css` (@theme inline `--font-sans`), `layout.tsx` (import and variable). 294→**Warning signs:** Body text rendering in system default sans-serif instead of DM Sans. 295→ 296→## Code Examples 297→ 298→### Example 1: globals.css Complete Theme Rewrite 299→```css 300→/* Source: shadcn theming docs + design-system.pen variables */ 301→@import "tailwindcss";    131→
   132→<frontmatter_guidance>
   133→**Purpose:** Enable automatic context assembly via dependency graph. Frontmatter makes summary metadata machine-readable so plan-phase can scan all summaries quickly and select relevant ones based on dependencies.
   134→
   135→**Fast scanning:** Frontmatter is first ~25 lines, cheap to scan across all summaries without reading full content.
   136→
   137→**Dependency graph:** `requires`/`provides`/`affects` create explicit links between phases, enabling transitive closure for context selection.
   138→
   139→**Subsystem:** Primary categorization (auth, payments, ui, api, database, infra, testing) for detecting related phases.
   140→
   141→**Tags:** Searchable technical keywords (libraries, frameworks, tools) for tech stack awareness.
   142→
   143→**Key-files:** Important files for @context references in PLAN.md.
   144→
   145→**Patterns:** Established conventions future phases should maintain.
   146→
   147→**Population:** Frontmatter is populated during summary creation in execute-plan.md. See `<step name="create_summary">` for field-by-field guidance.
   148→</frontmatter_guidance>
   149→
   150→<one_liner_rules>
   151→The one-liner MUST be substantive:
   152→
   153→**Good:**
   154→- "JWT auth with refresh rotation using jose library"
   155→- "Prisma schema with User, Session, and Product models"
   156→- "Dashboard with real-time metrics via Server-Sent Events"
   157→
   158→**Bad:**
   159→- "Phase complete"
   160→- "Authentication implemented"
   161→- "Foundation finished"
   162→- "All tasks done"
   163→
   164→The one-liner should tell someone what actually shipped.
   165→</one_liner_rules>
   166→
   167→<example>
   168→```markdown
   169→# Phase 1: Foundation Summary
   170→
   171→**JWT auth with refresh rotation using jose library, Prisma User model, and protected API middleware**
   172→
   173→## Performance
   174→
   175→- **Duration:** 28 min
   176→- **Started:** 2025-01-15T14:22:10Z
   177→- **Completed:** 2025-01-15T14:50:33Z
   178→- **Tasks:** 5
   179→- **Files modified:** 8
   180→
   181→## Accomplishments
   182→- User model with email/password auth
   183→- Login/logout endpoints with httpOnly JWT cookies
   184→- Protected route middleware checking token validity
   185→- Refresh token rotation on each request
   186→
   187→## Files Created/Modified
   188→- `prisma/schema.prisma` - User and Session models
   189→- `src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts` - Login endpoint
   190→- `src/app/api/auth/logout/route.ts` - Logout endpoint
   191→- `src/middleware.ts` - Protected route checks
   192→- `src/lib/auth.ts` - JWT helpers using jose
   193→
   194→## Decisions Made
   195→- Used jose instead of jsonwebtoken (ESM-native, Edge-compatible)
   196→- 15-min access tokens with 7-day refresh tokens
   197→- Storing refresh tokens in database for revocation capability
   198→
   199→## Deviations from Plan
   200→
   201→### Auto-fixed Issues
   202→
   203→**1. [Rule 2 - Missing Critical] Added password hashing with bcrypt**
   204→- **Found during:** Task 2 (Login endpoint implementation)
   205→- **Issue:** Plan didn't specify password hashing - storing plaintext would be critical security flaw
   206→- **Fix:** Added bcrypt hashing on registration, comparison on login with salt rounds 10
   207→- **Files modified:** src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts, src/lib/auth.ts
   208→- **Verification:** Password hash test passes, plaintext never stored
   209→- **Committed in:** abc123f (Task 2 commit)
   210→
   211→**2. [Rule 3 - Blocking] Installed missing jose dependency**
   212→- **Found during:** Task 4 (JWT token generation)
   213→- **Issue:** jose package not in package.json, import failing
   214→- **Fix:** Ran `npm install jose`
   215→- **Files modified:** package.json, package-lock.json
   216→- **Verification:** Import succeeds, build passes
   217→- **Committed in:** def456g (Task 4 commit)
   218→
   219→---
   220→
   221→**Total deviations:** 2 auto-fixed (1 missing critical, 1 blocking)
   222→**Impact on plan:** Both auto-fixes essential for security and functionality. No scope creep.
   223→
   224→## Issues Encountered
   225→- jsonwebtoken CommonJS import failed in Edge runtime - switched to jose (planned library change, worked as expected)
   226→
   227→## Next Phase Readiness
   228→- Auth foundation complete, ready for feature development
   229→- User registration endpoint needed before public launch
   230→
   231→---
   232→*Phase: 01-foundation*
   233→*Completed: 2025-01-15*
   234→```
   235→</example>
   236→
   237→<guidelines>
   238→**Frontmatter:** MANDATORY - complete all fields. Enables automatic context assembly for future planning.
   239→
   240→**One-liner:** Must be substantive. "JWT auth with refresh rotation using jose library" not "Authentication implemented".
   241→
   242→**Decisions section:**
   243→- Key decisions made during execution with rationale
   244→- Extracted to STATE.md accumulated context
   245→- Use "None - followed plan as specified" if no deviations
   246→
   247→**After creation:** STATE.md updated with position, decisions, issues.
   248→</guidelines>
   249→

# Phase 25.1 Plan 01: Theme/Globals Foundation Summary

**Blue-primary oklch color palette with DM Sans font and semantic status variables replacing default shadcn grayscale theme**

## Performance

- **Duration:** 2 min
- **Started:** 2026-03-01T17:40:17Z
- **Completed:** 2026-03-01T17:42:34Z
- **Tasks:** 3
- **Files modified:** 2

## Accomplishments
- Replaced default shadcn grayscale theme with design-system.pen blue-primary color palette (30+ oklch values)
- Added 13 semantic status Tailwind utility mappings (bg-success, text-error, bg-star, etc.) via @theme inline
- Swapped Geist Sans/Mono fonts for DM Sans (weights 400/500/600) via next/font/google
- Removed dark mode CSS section entirely (light-mode-only per design decision)
- Verified legacy pencil-new.pen is absent and all 11 design/*.pen split files are present

## Task Commits

Each task was committed atomically:

1. **Task 1: Rewrite globals.css with design color palette and semantic status variables** - `372043a7` (feat)
2. **Task 2: Swap Geist Sans for DM Sans in root layout** - `1431f552` (feat)
3. **Task 3: Verify legacy pencil-new.pen is absent** - no commit (guard task, no file changes)
4. **Formatting fix: Biome auto-format globals.css** - `1ec6a88b` (style)

## Files Created/Modified
- `src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/app/globals.css` - Full CSS variable theme rewrite with design-system.pen oklch colors, semantic status variables, light-mode only
- `src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/app/layout.tsx` - DM Sans font swap replacing Geist Sans/Mono, className moved to html tag

## Decisions Made
- Removed dark mode section entirely rather than keeping it inert -- cleaner CSS, avoids accidental activation
- Used exact oklch values from RESEARCH.md rather than re-converting from hex -- consistency with research phase
- Applied Biome auto-formatting to globals.css to remove extra whitespace alignment in comments

## Deviations from Plan

### Auto-fixed Issues

**1. [Rule 1 - Bug] Biome formatting on globals.css**
- **Found during:** Task 2 verification (lint check)
- **Issue:** CSS comments had extra whitespace alignment that Biome formatter flags as formatting error
- **Fix:** Ran `npx biome format --write` to normalize comment spacing
- **Files modified:** src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/app/globals.css
- **Verification:** `npx biome check src/app/globals.css` passes cleanly
- **Committed in:** 1ec6a88b (separate style commit)

---

**Total deviations:** 1 auto-fixed (1 formatting)
**Impact on plan:** Cosmetic-only formatting fix. No scope creep.

## Issues Encountered
- Pre-existing Biome lint errors (244 errors across 154 files) are unrelated to this plan's changes. The two modified files pass lint cleanly.

## User Setup Required
None - no external service configuration required.

## Next Phase Readiness
- Theme foundation is complete. All 10 subsequent screen plans can now use:
  - Blue-primary palette via `bg-primary`, `text-primary`, etc.
  - Semantic status utilities: `bg-success`, `text-error`, `bg-warning-bg`, `text-star`, etc.
  - DM Sans font renders on all pages via CSS variable chain
- No blockers for Plan 02 (Homepage)

    64→Recent decisions affecting v3.0:
    65→- **Aspire + K8s parallel paths**: Aspire stays as local inner-loop dev tool; Kustomize + ArgoCD define cluster state. No conflict.
    66→- **RabbitMQ as K8s transport**: Azure SB emulator cannot run in K8s; `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT` env var switches transport. Same consumer/saga/outbox config.
    67→- **Kustomize over Helm**: Simpler for single-project; no templating language; built into kubectl.
    68→- **ArgoCD v3.3.2 specifically**: v3.3.0/3.3.1 have a client-side apply migration bug.
    69→- **Sealed Secrets from day one**: No plaintext credentials in Git ever. Key backup in cluster bootstrap script.
    70→- **NodePort for kind access**: Ingress NGINX EOL March 2026; NodePort is simplest for local kind cluster.
    71→- **No MinIO**: Azurite not suitable for K8s; use placeholder images in K8s deployment.
    72→- [Phase 23]: Next.js Dockerfile uses AUTH_SECRET=placeholder-for-docker-build to prevent next-auth v5 build failures; real secret injected at K8s runtime
    73→- [Phase 23]: Use noble-chiseled-extra base image (not plain noble-chiseled) for .NET containers because InvariantGlobalization is not set
    74→- [Phase 23]: Separate container-images.yml workflow from release.yml -- GITHUB_TOKEN for ghcr.io auth, .NET multi-arch via per-arch publish + imagetools merge
    75→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: PGDATA subdirectory avoids initdb directory-not-empty error from kind local-path-provisioner
    76→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: Offset ports (3xxxx) for kind cluster avoid conflicts with Aspire local dev services
    77→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: Separate headless + NodePort services for PostgreSQL -- headless for StatefulSet DNS, NodePort for kind port mapping chain
    78→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Keycloak startup probe on management port 9000 with 15s initial delay and 60s probing window for slow first-boot realm import
    79→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Realm JSON copied from AppHost/Realms to K8s manifests for self-contained deployment; configMapGenerator for hash-suffixed ConfigMap
    80→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Separate NodePort services for kind external access; KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME/PASSWORD (not deprecated KEYCLOAK_ADMIN)
    81→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: SealedSecrets v0.27.3 pinned for reproducible controller install; sealed-secret.yaml generated dynamically by bootstrap script
    82→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: seal_secret helper function centralizes kubeseal invocation; dev defaults postgres/guest/admin for local-only kind cluster
    83→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: Bootstrap script idempotent (skips existing cluster); Keycloak 180s pod wait timeout for realm import
    84→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-04]: ROADMAP criterion #5 corrected from ApiService to Keycloak startup probe; ApiService probe deferred to Phase 25 criterion #6
    85→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT env var controls transport: RabbitMQ for K8s, AzureServiceBus (default) for Aspire
    86→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: NoOp DLQ service returns empty results in RabbitMQ mode; health endpoints unconditional for K8s probes
    87→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: CORS origins configurable via Cors:Origins config section with localhost fallback
    88→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: Env var substitution pattern: define secretKeyRef env vars BEFORE connection strings so K8s $(VAR) substitution works
    89→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: Gateway dual Service: ClusterIP for internal, NodePort 30800 for kind host access at 38800
    90→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: Web uses plaintext dev secrets matching Phase 23 Dockerfile approach; production would use SealedSecrets
    91→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: ApiService startup probe 160s window (10s initial + 30x5s) for EF Core migrations on first boot

content.text:
---

## Wave 1 Complete ✓

**25.1-01: Theme Foundation**
Blue primary oklch color palette (`#2563EB`), semantic status colors, and DM Sans font (400/500/600 weights) now set as CSS variables in `globals.css` and wired through `layout.tsx`. All 10 screen plans in Wave 2 will inherit this foundation.

---

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
 GSD ► EXECUTING WAVE 2
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

10 plans in parallel — all storefront pages, auth login, and admin dashboard.

**25.1-02: Homepage** — Gradient hero banner, category pills, featured product grid, restyled header/footer matching storefront-homepage.pen.

**25.1-03: Product Catalog** — Left sidebar filters (category checkboxes, price range slider, rating filter) with paginated product grid matching storefront-catalog.pen.

**25.1-04: Product Detail** — Image gallery, tabbed Description/Reviews content, QuantityStepper, breadcrumb nav, related products matching storefront-product-detail.pen.

**25.1-05: Shopping Cart** — Item table with quantity controls, order summary sidebar, checkout CTA matching storefront-cart.pen.

**25.1-06: Checkout Flow** — Step indicator, shipping/payment accordion sections, order summary sidebar matching storefront-checkout.pen.

**25.1-07: Order Confirmation** — Success banner, order details summary, next-action CTAs matching storefront-order-confirmation.pen.

**25.1-08: Order History** — Status badges, order timeline/stepper, clean table layout matching storefront-order-history.pen.

**25.1-09: User Profile** — Account sidebar navigation, profile form, avatar upload, address management matching storefront-profile.pen.

**25.1-10: Auth Login** — Branded login page wrapping Keycloak SSO redirect with storefront visual identity matching auth-login.pen.

**25.1-11: Admin Dashboard** — Dark navy sidebar layout with shadcn Sidebar, stat cards, product table, order kanban view matching admin-dashboard.pen.

◆ Spawning 10 agents in parallel...

## Execution Plan

**Phase 25.1: Update UI Based on Pencil Design with shadcn** — 11 plans across 2 waves

| Wave | Plans | What it builds |
|------|-------|----------------|
| 1 | 25.1-01 | ✓ Theme foundation (complete) |
| 2 | 25.1-02 thru 25.1-11 | All storefront pages, auth, and admin dashboard |

Found 11 plans (10 incomplete). Wave 1 already complete — skipping to Wave 2.

---

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
 GSD ► EXECUTING WAVE 2
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

10 plans in parallel — restyling every page in the storefront, auth, and admin.

**25.1-02: Homepage** — Gradient hero banner, category pills, featured product grid, restyled header/footer. Foundation for storefront identity.

**25.1-03: Product Catalog** — Left sidebar filters (category checkboxes, price slider, rating) with paginated product grid. Core browse experience.

**25.1-04: Product Detail** — Image gallery, tabbed Description/Reviews, QuantityStepper, breadcrumbs, related products. Key conversion page.

**25.1-05: Shopping Cart** — Item table with quantity controls, order summary sidebar, checkout CTA. Partial progress from prior session (cart item row).

**25.1-06: Checkout Flow** — Step indicator, shipping/payment accordion, order summary sidebar. Multi-section form layout.

**25.1-07: Order Confirmation** — Success banner, order details summary, next-action CTAs. Single-task plan.

**25.1-08: Order History** — Status badges, order timeline/stepper, clean table layout. List + detail views.

**25.1-09: User Profile** — Account sidebar nav, profile form, avatar upload, address management. Partial progress (sidebar/layout).

**25.1-10: Auth Login** — Branded Keycloak SSO login page with storefront visual identity. Partial progress (login page created).

**25.1-11: Admin Dashboard** — Dark navy sidebar, stat cards, product table, order kanban view. Largest plan (3 tasks).

◆ Spawning 10 agents in parallel...

# Requirements: MicroCommerce v3.0

**Defined:** 2026-02-25
**Core Value:** A user can complete a purchase end-to-end — now deployed to Kubernetes via GitOps

## v3.0 Requirements

Requirements for Kubernetes & GitOps deployment milestone. Each maps to roadmap phases.

### Containerization

- [x] **CONT-01**: ApiService has a multi-stage Dockerfile with layer-cached restore and chiseled runtime image
- [x] **CONT-02**: Gateway has a multi-stage Dockerfile with layer-cached restore and chiseled runtime image
- [x] **CONT-03**: Web (Next.js) has a multi-stage Dockerfile with standalone output and node:alpine runtime
- [x] **CONT-04**: All 3 images are pushed to ghcr.io with SHA-based tags

### K8s Infrastructure

- [x] **INFRA-01**: PostgreSQL runs as a StatefulSet with PersistentVolumeClaim in the kind cluster
- [x] **INFRA-02**: RabbitMQ runs as a Deployment with Service in the kind cluster
- [x] **INFRA-03**: Keycloak runs as a Deployment with realm JSON imported via ConfigMap
- [x] **INFRA-04**: kind cluster can be created from a config file with port mappings for local access
- [x] **INFRA-05**: Startup probes protect Keycloak (Phase 24) and ApiService (Phase 25) from premature liveness failures

### Application Manifests

- [x] **MFST-01**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web have Deployment + Service + ConfigMap manifests
- [x] **MFST-02**: All app services have liveness (`/alive`) and readiness (`/health`) probes configured
- [x] **MFST-03**: All containers have CPU and memory resource requests and limits
- [x] **MFST-04**: Kustomize base directory contains all environment-neutral manifests
- [x] **MFST-05**: Kustomize dev overlay patches image tags and resource limits for kind
- [x] **MFST-06**: All resources are namespaced under `micro-commerce`

### MassTransit Transport

- [x] **TRAN-01**: MassTransit supports RabbitMQ transport in K8s deployments
- [x] **TRAN-02**: Transport selection is configurable via `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT` env var (Azure SB default, RabbitMQ for K8s)

### CI/CD Pipeline

- [x] **CICD-01**: GitHub Actions workflow builds and pushes all 3 images to ghcr.io on push to master
- [ ] **CICD-02**: CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay, triggering ArgoCD sync

### GitOps

- [ ] **GOPS-01**: ArgoCD is installed in the kind cluster and manages all services
- [ ] **GOPS-02**: App-of-apps root Application manages per-service child Applications
- [x] **GOPS-03**: Sealed Secrets controller encrypts all credentials; SealedSecret YAMLs generated by bootstrap script and committed to Git after first run

### Observability

- [ ] **OBSV-01**: OTEL Collector receives OTLP from all app services and forwards to Aspire Dashboard
- [ ] **OBSV-02**: Aspire Dashboard runs as a standalone container in K8s accessible for dev monitoring

## Future Requirements

Deferred to post-v3.0. Tracked but not in current roadmap.

### Scaling & Hardening

- **SCALE-01**: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler with metrics-based scaling
- **SCALE-02**: Network Policies for pod-to-pod traffic control
- **SCALE-03**: cert-manager with TLS termination at ingress
- **SCALE-04**: PostgreSQL Operator (CloudNativePG) for HA database

### Multi-Environment

- **ENV-01**: Staging overlay for pre-production validation
- **ENV-02**: Production overlay with production-grade resource limits
- **ENV-03**: Multi-cluster deployment with ArgoCD

### Storage

- **STOR-01**: MinIO for Blob Storage replacement in K8s (avatar/product images)

## Out of Scope

| Feature | Reason |
|---------|--------|
| Service Mesh (Istio/Linkerd) | YARP Gateway already centralizes auth/routing; massive operational complexity |
| Helm charts | Kustomize is simpler for single-project, built into kubectl |
| External Secrets Operator | Requires external vault; Sealed Secrets works offline with kind |
| FluxCD | ArgoCD already chosen; switching tools is waste |
| Multi-environment overlays | Only dev cluster exists; structure repo for easy addition later |
| Ingress NGINX | EOL March 2026; use NodePort for kind local access |
| Azure Blob Storage in K8s | Azurite emulator not suitable for K8s; use placeholder images |

### Phase 25.1: Update UI based on pencil design with shadcn (INSERTED)

**Goal:** Refresh all storefront and admin UI screens to match the split per-page pencil design files using existing shadcn/ui primitives and Tailwind CSS. Visual update only -- no new backend features, API endpoints, or routes.
**Requirements**: UI-THEME, UI-HOME, UI-CATALOG, UI-PRODUCT-DETAIL, UI-CART, UI-CHECKOUT, UI-ORDER-CONFIRM, UI-ORDER-HISTORY, UI-PROFILE, UI-AUTH-LOGIN, UI-ADMIN
**Depends on:** Phase 25
**Plans:** 11/11 plans complete

Plans:
- [x] 25.1-01-PLAN.md -- Theme/globals: CSS variable color palette + DM Sans font swap
- [x] 25.1-02-PLAN.md -- Homepage: hero, category pills, product cards, header, footer
- [x] 25.1-03-PLAN.md -- Product Catalog: sidebar filters, product grid, pagination
- [x] 25.1-04-PLAN.md -- Product Detail: image gallery, tabs, quantity stepper, reviews
- [x] 25.1-05-PLAN.md -- Shopping Cart: item rows, cart summary sidebar
- [x] 25.1-06-PLAN.md -- Checkout: step indicator, shipping/payment forms, order sidebar
- [x] 25.1-07-PLAN.md -- Order Confirmation: success banner, order details
- [x] 25.1-08-PLAN.md -- Order History: order list table, detail with status stepper
- [x] 25.1-09-PLAN.md -- User Profile: account sidebar, profile form, addresses
- [x] 25.1-10-PLAN.md -- Auth Login: branded Keycloak login page
- [x] 25.1-11-PLAN.md -- Admin Dashboard: dark sidebar layout, stat cards, tables

### Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps
**Goal**: ArgoCD manages all cluster resources from Git using app-of-apps, replacing manual kubectl apply
**Depends on**: Phase 25
**Requirements**: GOPS-01, GOPS-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. ArgoCD UI is reachable in the kind cluster and shows all services as Synced and Healthy
  2. Deleting a deployment manually causes ArgoCD to automatically restore it within one sync cycle
  3. The app-of-apps root Application manages each service as an independent child Application
**Plans**: TBD

### Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure
**Goal**: A push to master automatically flows through CI image build, Git tag commit, and ArgoCD cluster rollout without manual intervention
**Depends on**: Phase 26
**Requirements**: CICD-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. After a push to master, a new pod with the updated SHA-tagged image is running in the cluster within 5 minutes of the CI workflow completing
  2. Every deployment is traceable to a Git commit — the image tag in the overlay matches the commit SHA that triggered the build
**Plans**: TBD

### Phase 28: Observability
**Goal**: Traces, metrics, and logs from all cluster services are visible in the Aspire Dashboard without requiring .NET Aspire AppHost
**Depends on**: Phase 27
**Requirements**: OBSV-01, OBSV-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. The Aspire Dashboard UI is reachable in the kind cluster and shows live traces from ApiService and Gateway
  2. A completed checkout flow produces a distributed trace visible end-to-end in the Dashboard spanning ApiService, the saga, and RabbitMQ consumers
  3. OTEL Collector pod stays healthy under normal load (no OOM kills, no dropped telemetry in logs)
**Plans**: TBD

### Human Verification Required

#### 1. ProductCard Add to Cart (Post-Fix)

**Test:** On homepage or catalog, click "Add to Cart" button on any product card
**Expected:** Cart badge increments and a success toast appears (not "Cart coming soon!")
**Why human:** Requires runtime mutation execution and UI feedback observation

#### 2. Hero Banner Visual Quality

**Test:** Open homepage and inspect the hero section gradient
**Expected:** Gradient is visually distinct blue-to-green (even if soft tints), not plain white/gray
**Why human:** Color subtlety cannot be assessed programmatically

#### 3. Checkout Step Progression

**Test:** Add an item to cart, go to checkout, fill shipping form, click Continue
**Expected:** Step indicator transitions from Shipping (active) to Payment (active), Shipping shows as completed with check
**Why human:** Interactive state transitions require browser testing

#### 4. Admin Sidebar Dark Theme Isolation

**Test:** Visit /admin, observe dark navy sidebar; then visit storefront, observe light background
**Expected:** Dark sidebar colors do not bleed into storefront Sheet or other light UI elements
**Why human:** CSS variable scoping correctness requires visual cross-page inspection

---

### Gaps Summary

**One gap blocks full goal achievement for UI-HOME:**

The `ProductCard` component — rendered on the homepage and catalog grid — shows `toast("Cart coming soon!")` on both Add-to-Cart buttons (hover overlay and card footer button). The `useAddToCart` hook is already correctly imported and wired in `product-detail.tsx`, confirming the hook is available. The fix is straightforward: import `useAddToCart` in `product-card.tsx` and replace the two toast stubs with `addToCart.mutate({ productId: product.id, quantity: 1 })`.

All other 27 must-haves are fully verified. The theme system (Plan 01), catalog UI (Plan 03), product detail (Plan 04), cart (Plan 05), checkout (Plan 06), order confirmation (Plan 07), order history (Plan 08), profile (Plan 09), login (Plan 10), and admin (Plan 11) are all substantive and correctly wired.

    70→## Accumulated Context
    71→
    72→### Decisions
    73→
    74→Decisions are logged in PROJECT.md Key Decisions table.
    75→
    76→Recent decisions affecting v3.0:
    77→- **Aspire + K8s parallel paths**: Aspire stays as local inner-loop dev tool; Kustomize + ArgoCD define cluster state. No conflict.
    78→- **RabbitMQ as K8s transport**: Azure SB emulator cannot run in K8s; `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT` env var switches transport. Same consumer/saga/outbox config.
    79→- **Kustomize over Helm**: Simpler for single-project; no templating language; built into kubectl.
    80→- **ArgoCD v3.3.2 specifically**: v3.3.0/3.3.1 have a client-side apply migration bug.
    81→- **Sealed Secrets from day one**: No plaintext credentials in Git ever. Key backup in cluster bootstrap script.
    82→- **NodePort for kind access**: Ingress NGINX EOL March 2026; NodePort is simplest for local kind cluster.
    83→- **No MinIO**: Azurite not suitable for K8s; use placeholder images in K8s deployment.
    84→- [Phase 23]: Next.js Dockerfile uses AUTH_SECRET=placeholder-for-docker-build to prevent next-auth v5 build failures; real secret injected at K8s runtime
    85→- [Phase 23]: Use noble-chiseled-extra base image (not plain noble-chiseled) for .NET containers because InvariantGlobalization is not set
    86→- [Phase 23]: Separate container-images.yml workflow from release.yml -- GITHUB_TOKEN for ghcr.io auth, .NET multi-arch via per-arch publish + imagetools merge
    87→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: PGDATA subdirectory avoids initdb directory-not-empty error from kind local-path-provisioner
    88→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: Offset ports (3xxxx) for kind cluster avoid conflicts with Aspire local dev services
    89→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-01]: Separate headless + NodePort services for PostgreSQL -- headless for StatefulSet DNS, NodePort for kind port mapping chain
    90→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Keycloak startup probe on management port 9000 with 15s initial delay and 60s probing window for slow first-boot realm import
    91→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Realm JSON copied from AppHost/Realms to K8s manifests for self-contained deployment; configMapGenerator for hash-suffixed ConfigMap
    92→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-02]: Separate NodePort services for kind external access; KC_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_USERNAME/PASSWORD (not deprecated KEYCLOAK_ADMIN)
    93→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: SealedSecrets v0.27.3 pinned for reproducible controller install; sealed-secret.yaml generated dynamically by bootstrap script
    94→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: seal_secret helper function centralizes kubeseal invocation; dev defaults postgres/guest/admin for local-only kind cluster
    95→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-03]: Bootstrap script idempotent (skips existing cluster); Keycloak 180s pod wait timeout for realm import
    96→- [Phase 24]: [Phase 24-04]: ROADMAP criterion #5 corrected from ApiService to Keycloak startup probe; ApiService probe deferred to Phase 25 criterion #6
    97→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT env var controls transport: RabbitMQ for K8s, AzureServiceBus (default) for Aspire
    98→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: NoOp DLQ service returns empty results in RabbitMQ mode; health endpoints unconditional for K8s probes
    99→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-01]: CORS origins configurable via Cors:Origins config section with localhost fallback
   100→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: Env var substitution pattern: define secretKeyRef env vars BEFORE connection strings so K8s $(VAR) substitution works
   101→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: Gateway dual Service: ClusterIP for internal, NodePort 30800 for kind host access at 38800
   102→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: Web uses plaintext dev secrets matching Phase 23 Dockerfile approach; production would use SealedSecrets
   103→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-02]: ApiService startup probe 160s window (10s initial + 30x5s) for EF Core migrations on first boot
   104→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-03]: Infrastructure-first bootstrap: Step 7 applies base/ for infra, Step 11 applies overlays/dev/ for full stack (idempotent)
   105→- [Phase 25]: [Phase 25-03]: ContainerRegistry='' overrides ghcr.io default for local-only kind images; dotnet publish /t:PublishContainer for .NET, docker build for Web
   106→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-01]: Removed dark mode section entirely (not kept inert) per locked design decision
   107→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-01]: Used exact oklch values from RESEARCH.md for design-system.pen color consistency
   108→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-01]: Biome auto-formatted comment alignment in globals.css (cosmetic only)
   109→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-10]: Email/password fields as disabled visual chrome; actual auth via Keycloak SSO redirect
   110→- [Phase 25.1]: [Phase 25.1-10]: Both Sign In and SSO buttons trigger signIn('keycloak') for consistent flow

    58→### 🚧 v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps (In Progress)
    59→
    60→**Milestone Goal:** Deploy the full MicroCommerce stack to a kind-based Kubernetes cluster using GitOps via ArgoCD, with GitHub Actions CI building and pushing images to ghcr.io, Kustomize managing manifests, and Sealed Secrets handling credentials safely in Git.
    61→
    62→- [x] **Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline** - Build production-ready images for all three services and push them to ghcr.io via GitHub Actions (completed 2026-02-25)
    63→- [x] **Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets** - Deploy PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak in the kind cluster with Sealed Secrets for all credentials (completed 2026-02-26)
    64→- [x] **Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport** - Deploy ApiService, Gateway, and Web with Kustomize base/overlay structure and RabbitMQ transport support (completed 2026-02-26)
    65→- [ ] **Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps** - Install ArgoCD and wire app-of-apps to manage all cluster resources from Git
    66→- [ ] **Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure** - Extend CI to commit SHA image tags back to the overlay, completing the full GitOps loop
    67→- [ ] **Phase 28: Observability** - Deploy OTEL Collector and standalone Aspire Dashboard for in-cluster monitoring
    68→
    69→## Phase Details
    70→
    71→### Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline
    72→**Goal**: Three production-ready container images exist in ghcr.io and are built automatically on every push to master
    73→**Depends on**: Phase 22 (v2.0 complete)
    74→**Requirements**: CONT-01, CONT-02, CONT-03, CONT-04, CICD-01
    75→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    76→  1. `docker build` succeeds for ApiService, Gateway, and Web with no warnings or root-user violations
    77→  2. `docker run` starts each service and the process responds to health checks within 30 seconds
    78→  3. A push to master triggers the GitHub Actions workflow and all three images appear in ghcr.io tagged with the commit SHA
    79→  4. Image sizes are reasonable: .NET images use chiseled runtime and Next.js uses standalone output with node:alpine
    80→**Plans**: TBD
    81→
    82→### Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets
    83→**Goal**: PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak run stably in the kind cluster with all credentials sealed in Git
    84→**Depends on**: Phase 23
    85→**Requirements**: INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03, INFRA-04, INFRA-05, GOPS-03
    86→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    87→  1. `kind create cluster --config kind-config.yaml` creates a cluster and all infrastructure pods reach Running state
    88→  2. PostgreSQL data persists across pod restarts (PVC-backed StatefulSet)
    89→  3. Keycloak admin UI is reachable and the MicroCommerce realm is pre-loaded from a ConfigMap
    90→  4. No plaintext credentials exist in any committed YAML file; all secrets are SealedSecret objects
    91→  5. Keycloak startup probe is configured with sufficient timeout (15s delay + 60s probing window) to survive slow first-boot realm import
    92→**Plans**: 4 plans
    93→- [x] 24-01-PLAN.md -- Kind cluster config, Kustomize scaffold, PostgreSQL StatefulSet
    94→- [x] 24-02-PLAN.md -- RabbitMQ Deployment, Keycloak Deployment with realm import
    95→- [x] 24-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script with SealedSecrets integration
    96→- [x] 24-04-PLAN.md -- Gap closure: fix ROADMAP misalignment on success criterion #5
    97→
    98→### Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport
    99→**Goal**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web are deployed via Kustomize and communicate correctly using K8s DNS and RabbitMQ messaging
   100→**Depends on**: Phase 24
   101→**Requirements**: MFST-01, MFST-02, MFST-03, MFST-04, MFST-05, MFST-06, TRAN-01, TRAN-02
   102→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   103→  1. The storefront loads in a browser via the Gateway NodePort — browsing products and adding to cart works end-to-end
   104→  2. Checkout completes successfully using the RabbitMQ transport (saga orchestrates stock reservation and mock payment)
   105→  3. All pods report liveness and readiness as healthy (`kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce` shows all Running/Ready)
   106→  4. All resources are in the `micro-commerce` namespace and have CPU/memory resource limits set
   107→  5. Setting `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT=AzureServiceBus` (Aspire dev path) and `RabbitMQ` (K8s path) both produce working configurations
   108→  6. ApiService startup probe prevents liveness failure during slow first-boot EF Core migration (deferred from Phase 24 INFRA-05)
   109→**Plans**: 3 plans
   110→- [x] 25-01-PLAN.md -- MassTransit RabbitMQ transport switching, health endpoint fix, conditional Azure SDK
   111→- [x] 25-02-PLAN.md -- ApiService, Gateway, Web K8s Deployment/Service manifests, Kustomize base/overlay
   112→- [x] 25-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script extension with app image build, kind load, and full stack deployment
   113→

   134→### Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps
   135→**Goal**: ArgoCD manages all cluster resources from Git using app-of-apps, replacing manual kubectl apply
   136→**Depends on**: Phase 25
   137→**Requirements**: GOPS-01, GOPS-02
   138→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   139→  1. ArgoCD UI is reachable in the kind cluster and shows all services as Synced and Healthy
   140→  2. Deleting a deployment manually causes ArgoCD to automatically restore it within one sync cycle
   141→  3. The app-of-apps root Application manages each service as an independent child Application
   142→**Plans**: TBD
   143→
   144→### Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure
   145→**Goal**: A push to master automatically flows through CI image build, Git tag commit, and ArgoCD cluster rollout without manual intervention
   146→**Depends on**: Phase 26
   147→**Requirements**: CICD-02
   148→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   149→  1. After a push to master, a new pod with the updated SHA-tagged image is running in the cluster within 5 minutes of the CI workflow completing
   150→  2. Every deployment is traceable to a Git commit — the image tag in the overlay matches the commit SHA that triggered the build
   151→**Plans**: TBD
   152→
   153→### Phase 28: Observability
   154→**Goal**: Traces, metrics, and logs from all cluster services are visible in the Aspire Dashboard without requiring .NET Aspire AppHost
   155→**Depends on**: Phase 27
   156→**Requirements**: OBSV-01, OBSV-02
   157→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   158→  1. The Aspire Dashboard UI is reachable in the kind cluster and shows live traces from ApiService and Gateway
   159→  2. A completed checkout flow produces a distributed trace visible end-to-end in the Dashboard spanning ApiService, the saga, and RabbitMQ consumers
   160→  3. OTEL Collector pod stays healthy under normal load (no OOM kills, no dropped telemetry in logs)
   161→**Plans**: TBD

1→# Requirements: MicroCommerce v3.0
     2→
     3→**Defined:** 2026-02-25
     4→**Core Value:** A user can complete a purchase end-to-end — now deployed to Kubernetes via GitOps
     5→
     6→## v3.0 Requirements
     7→
     8→Requirements for Kubernetes & GitOps deployment milestone. Each maps to roadmap phases.
     9→
    10→### Containerization
    11→
    12→- [x] **CONT-01**: ApiService has a multi-stage Dockerfile with layer-cached restore and chiseled runtime image
    13→- [x] **CONT-02**: Gateway has a multi-stage Dockerfile with layer-cached restore and chiseled runtime image
    14→- [x] **CONT-03**: Web (Next.js) has a multi-stage Dockerfile with standalone output and node:alpine runtime
    15→- [x] **CONT-04**: All 3 images are pushed to ghcr.io with SHA-based tags
    16→
    17→### K8s Infrastructure
    18→
    19→- [x] **INFRA-01**: PostgreSQL runs as a StatefulSet with PersistentVolumeClaim in the kind cluster
    20→- [x] **INFRA-02**: RabbitMQ runs as a Deployment with Service in the kind cluster
    21→- [x] **INFRA-03**: Keycloak runs as a Deployment with realm JSON imported via ConfigMap
    22→- [x] **INFRA-04**: kind cluster can be created from a config file with port mappings for local access
    23→- [x] **INFRA-05**: Startup probes protect Keycloak (Phase 24) and ApiService (Phase 25) from premature liveness failures
    24→
    25→### Application Manifests
    26→
    27→- [x] **MFST-01**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web have Deployment + Service + ConfigMap manifests
    28→- [x] **MFST-02**: All app services have liveness (`/alive`) and readiness (`/health`) probes configured
    29→- [x] **MFST-03**: All containers have CPU and memory resource requests and limits
    30→- [x] **MFST-04**: Kustomize base directory contains all environment-neutral manifests
    31→- [x] **MFST-05**: Kustomize dev overlay patches image tags and resource limits for kind
    32→- [x] **MFST-06**: All resources are namespaced under `micro-commerce`
    33→
    34→### MassTransit Transport
    35→
    36→- [x] **TRAN-01**: MassTransit supports RabbitMQ transport in K8s deployments
    37→- [x] **TRAN-02**: Transport selection is configurable via `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT` env var (Azure SB default, RabbitMQ for K8s)
    38→
    39→### CI/CD Pipeline
    40→
    41→- [x] **CICD-01**: GitHub Actions workflow builds and pushes all 3 images to ghcr.io on push to master
    42→- [ ] **CICD-02**: CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay, triggering ArgoCD sync
    43→
    44→### GitOps
    45→
    46→- [ ] **GOPS-01**: ArgoCD is installed in the kind cluster and manages all services
    47→- [ ] **GOPS-02**: App-of-apps root Application manages per-service child Applications
    48→- [x] **GOPS-03**: Sealed Secrets controller encrypts all credentials; SealedSecret YAMLs generated by bootstrap script and committed to Git after first run
    49→
    50→### Observability
    51→
    52→- [ ] **OBSV-01**: OTEL Collector receives OTLP from all app services and forwards to Aspire Dashboard
    53→- [ ] **OBSV-02**: Aspire Dashboard runs as a standalone container in K8s accessible for dev monitoring
    54→
    55→## Future Requirements
    56→
    57→Deferred to post-v3.0. Tracked but not in current roadmap.
    58→
    59→### Scaling & Hardening
    60→
    61→- **SCALE-01**: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler with metrics-based scaling
    62→- **SCALE-02**: Network Policies for pod-to-pod traffic control
    63→- **SCALE-03**: cert-manager with TLS termination at ingress
    64→- **SCALE-04**: PostgreSQL Operator (CloudNativePG) for HA database
    65→
    66→### Multi-Environment
    67→
    68→- **ENV-01**: Staging overlay for pre-production validation
    69→- **ENV-02**: Production overlay with production-grade resource limits
    70→- **ENV-03**: Multi-cluster deployment with ArgoCD
    71→
    72→### Storage
    73→
    74→- **STOR-01**: MinIO for Blob Storage replacement in K8s (avatar/product images)
    75→
    76→## Out of Scope
    77→
    78→| Feature | Reason |
    79→|---------|--------|
    80→| Service Mesh (Istio/Linkerd) | YARP Gateway already centralizes auth/routing; massive operational complexity |
    81→| Helm charts | Kustomize is simpler for single-project, built into kubectl |
    82→| External Secrets Operator | Requires external vault; Sealed Secrets works offline with kind |
    83→| FluxCD | ArgoCD already chosen; switching tools is waste |
    84→| Multi-environment overlays | Only dev cluster exists; structure repo for easy addition later |
    85→| Ingress NGINX | EOL March 2026; use NodePort for kind local access |
    86→| Azure Blob Storage in K8s | Azurite emulator not suitable for K8s; use placeholder images |

1→# Phase 25.1: Update UI based on pencil design with shadcn - Context
     2→
     3→**Gathered:** 2026-03-02
     4→**Status:** Ready for planning
     5→
     6→<domain>
     7→## Phase Boundary
     8→
     9→Refresh all storefront and admin UI screens to match the split per-page pencil design files using existing shadcn/ui primitives and Tailwind CSS. This is a visual update of existing screens — no new backend features, API endpoints, or routes are added. The `design/*.pen` files are the source of truth for layout, colors, and component styling.
    10→
    11→</domain>
    12→
    13→<decisions>
    14→## Implementation Decisions
    15→
    16→### Design file strategy
    17→- Source of truth: per-page `.pen` files in `design/` directory (11 files total)
    18→- `design/design-system.pen` for shared component specs (26 reusable components) and color variables
    19→- Each screen references its own `.pen` file (e.g., `design/storefront-homepage.pen` for homepage)
    20→- Old monolithic `pencil-new.pen` is deleted — split files replace it entirely
    21→- Design fidelity: match layout structure, color palette, component style from pencil. Use shadcn defaults for spacing/radius where close enough. Don't chase pixel-perfect
    22→
    23→### Component strategy
    24→- Use shadcn/ui for standard components (Button, Badge, Input, Table, Avatar, Tabs, etc.) — theme them to match design
    25→- Create custom components only for things shadcn doesn't cover: CategoryPill, StarRating, QuantityStepper, StepIndicator, ProductCard
    26→- Custom feature components in `src/components/storefront/`, reusable primitives in `src/components/ui/`
    27→- Rewrite existing storefront components to match design — keep same file names and prop interfaces where possible, JSX/styling gets fresh start
    28→- Install additional shadcn components as needed per screen (e.g., tabs, pagination, breadcrumb). Don't pre-install everything
    29→
    30→### Font & typography
    31→- Primary font: DM Sans via next/font/google (matches design-system.pen exactly)
    32→- Fallback: Inter, then system sans-serif
    33→- Load weights 400 (normal), 500 (medium), 600 (semibold) only — matches design usage, keeps bundle small
    34→- Single font stack for entire app — no separate display font for headings
    35→- Replace current Geist Sans references with DM Sans
    36→
    37→### Color theme migration
    38→- Convert design hex colors to oklch format in globals.css — maintains Tailwind v4 + shadcn best practice
    39→- Primary blue: #2563EB (oklch equivalent), foreground: #1A1A1A, border: #E5E7EB, etc.
    40→- Extend CSS variables with semantic status colors: --success, --success-bg, --success-foreground (and warning/error/info equivalents)
    41→- Add --star (#FBBF24 amber) as a theme variable for star ratings
    42→- Admin sidebar: override --sidebar-bg (#111827), --sidebar-foreground (#F9FAFB), --sidebar-accent (#1F2937) via CSS custom properties
    43→- No dark mode — light mode only. Design only shows light mode
    44→- Remove .dark section from globals.css (or keep inert)
    45→
    46→### Screen priority & scope
    47→- All 10 screens + theme/globals setup = 11 implementation units
    48→- One plan per screen for clear progress tracking
    49→- Implementation order (customer journey):
    50→  1. Theme/globals (CSS variables, font, shared component theming)
    51→  2. Homepage (`design/storefront-homepage.pen`)
    52→  3. Product Catalog (`design/storefront-catalog.pen`)
    53→  4. Product Detail (`design/storefront-product-detail.pen`)
    54→  5. Shopping Cart (`design/storefront-cart.pen`)
    55→  6. Checkout (`design/storefront-checkout.pen`)
    56→  7. Order Confirmation (`design/storefront-order-confirmation.pen`)
    57→  8. Order History (`design/storefront-order-history.pen`)
    58→  9. User Profile (`design/storefront-profile.pen`)
    59→  10. Auth Login (`design/auth-login.pen`)
    60→  11. Admin Dashboard (`design/admin-dashboard.pen`)
    61→
    62→### Responsive approach
    63→- Desktop-first: build 1440px layout from pencil, add sensible breakpoints
    64→- Stack grids to single column, collapse sidebar to sheet/drawer, adjust padding
    65→- Admin sidebar: shared layout component in admin `layout.tsx` as persistent navigation shell
    66→
    67→### Claude's Discretion
    68→- Exact responsive breakpoint values and mobile layout adjustments
    69→- Loading/skeleton states per screen
    70→- Error state handling
    71→- Animation/transitions between states
    72→- Exact shadcn component selection per screen
    73→- Which components are "reusable primitives" (ui/) vs "feature components" (storefront/)
    74→- Per-component decision on refactor vs rewrite based on complexity
    75→- Exact oklch conversion values for each hex color
    76→
    77→</decisions>

The work is purely presentational -- no new backend features, API endpoints, or routes are added. Existing React component file names and prop interfaces are preserved where possible; only the JSX/styling gets rewritten. 59→ 60→The current codebase uses Next.js 16.0.3, React 19.2, Tailwind CSS v4.1.17, and shadcn/ui (new-york style, v3.8.5 CLI). There are 18 shadcn/ui components already installed, 23 storefront components, and a well-structured app router layout. The main changes are: (1) swap the color system from default shadcn grayscale to the design's blue-primary palette in oklch, (2) replace Geist Sans with DM Sans font, (3) add custom semantic status variables, (4) add missing shadcn components (tabs, pagination, breadcrumb, checkbox, slider, sidebar), and (5) rewrite each screen's JSX to match the design layout. 61→ 62→**Primary recommendation:** Implement as 11 sequential plans (theme first, then each screen in customer-journey order). Each plan reads its `.pen` design file, installs any needed shadcn components, and rewrites the screen's components to match the design.
